headline: AMD stock climbs after Microsoft expands Azure AI partnership
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) shares gained 5% on Monday after Microsoft announced a major expansion of its strategic partnership with the chipmaker, positioning AMD’s latest AI hardware as a key component of Azure’s next-generation cloud infrastructure. At the centre of the agreement is Microsoft’s large-scale adoption of the AMD Helios Rackscale Solution, which will support frontier AI model inference across Azure cloud services and enterprise workloads.

A combination of Microsoft and AMD technology will power new tools for data processing, electronic design automation, and inference, or running AI models.

headline: Magnificent Seven Dominate S&P 500 Earnings, But That's Changing Dramatically
summary: The Magnificent Seven will again reign supreme over S&P 500 earnings, but their dominance is fading. headline: Markets may get 'increasingly nervous' if hyperscalers raise AI capex further
summary: US stocks (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) are mixed ahead of Monday's close as investors await the release of second quarter earnings from Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Tesla (TSLA), Intel (INTC), and IBM (IBM) throughout this week. Tematica Research CIO Chris Versace comes on Market Domination to comment on how markets may end up reacting to AI spending forecasts outlined in these upcoming earnings.

headline: Wall Street drifts as AI stocks hold steadier after last week’s losses
summary: Wall Street drifted to a quiet finish Monday after stocks of chipmakers and other winners of the artificial-intelligence boom trimmed some of their recent losses.  The S&P 500 dipped 0.2%, coming off its first losing week in the last three and just its third since the end of March.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 307 points, or 0.6%, and the Nasdaq composite was basically flat after slipping by less than 0.1%.

content.title: Wall Street drifts as AI stocks hold steadier after last week’s losses
content.summary: Wall Street drifted to a quiet finish Monday after stocks of chipmakers and other winners of the artificial-intelligence boom trimmed some of their recent losses.  The S&P 500 dipped 0.2%, coming off its first losing week in the last three and just its third since the end of March.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped 307 points, or 0.6%, and the Nasdaq composite was basically flat after slipping by less than 0.1%.
